title: Investor Helpdesk Agent
problem: Wealth advisors field repetitive investor questions that require pulling from CRM history, product knowledge bases, and compliance-approved language — each from different systems.
solution: A coordinator dispatches two specialized subagents in parallel (CRM-LLM for account history, Product-LLM for investment knowledge), merges their typed results, and a Formatter-LLM produces a compliant, personalized response.
whoItsFor: Wealth Advisors, Client Service Teams at RIAs and Broker-Dealers
workflowSteps: Coordinator identifies query type and dispatches parallel subagents
CRM-LLM: retrieves account history, past interactions, investor preferences
Product-LLM: retrieves relevant fund/product docs from knowledge base
Coordinator merges {status, result} from each subagent
Formatter-LLM: composes compliant response matching house tone and regulatory disclosure requirements
keyInsights: This is the clearest example of the multi-LLM coordinator pattern in the document. Each subagent is specialized (knows its data domain well), and the formatter is the compliance guardrail. The typed merge contract between subagents and coordinator is what prevents hallucinated cross-data blending.

relatedExams.why: Solution Design & Architecture — parallel agent topologies

mermaidDiagram:
flowchart LR
  A([Investor Query]) --> B[Coordinator LLM]
  B --> C1[CRM-LLM: Account History]
  B --> C2[Product-LLM: Investment Docs]
  C1 & C2 --> D[Typed Merge]
  D --> E[Formatter-LLM: Compliance Language]
  E --> F([Compliant Personalized Response])
architectureNotes: A RAG pipeline retrieves relevant chunks from fund fact sheets, prospectuses, and performance reports stored in a vector database. The retrieval context is injected into the LLM prompt, which produces a grounded, cited answer. An eligibility filter runs pre-retrieval to restrict content by investor tier. A reranker prioritises the most topically relevant chunks when the query spans multiple funds.

failureModes.mode: Answer cites wrong fund performance figures
failureModes.mitigation: Ground answer strictly to retrieved chunks — no parametric knowledge for financial data; add citation check that each figure in the answer has a source reference.

failureModes.mode: Entitlement filter bypass serves restricted fund data
failureModes.mitigation: Pre-filter applied at vector search time (WHERE investor_tier IN (…)), not post-retrieval; security audit on every retrieval path change.

failureModes.mode: Embedding index staleness after NAV update
failureModes.mitigation: Event-driven re-embed on document publish event (not scheduled batch); use document version hash to detect changes and trigger incremental re-indexing.

failureModes.mode: Reranker latency causes P99 > 3s for complex queries
failureModes.mitigation: Cap reranking to top-20 candidates; apply async prefetch on common query patterns; cache reranker scores for repeated query/document pairs.

scalingConsiderations: Vector index size: at 10,000 fund documents × 500 chunks/doc = 5M vectors; pgvector handles this with HNSW index; Pinecone recommended above 50M vectors.
Embedding refresh cadence: NAV and performance data updates daily — use change-data-capture to trigger incremental re-embed rather than full re-index.
Latency SLA: wealth management investors expect < 2s response; pre-compute embeddings for top-100 recurring query patterns as cache layer.
Multi-tenancy: isolate vector namespaces per fund family to prevent cross-contamination of proprietary product data.

integrations.note: Source of fund fact sheets, prospectuses, performance reports
Investor tier and entitlement data for RBAC pre-filter
Real-time NAV and benchmark data for freshness check
Web UI or chatbot widget exposing the Q&A interface
All queries and retrieved chunks logged for compliance
